meaning: n.严酷的考验,痛苦的经验,折磨
examples: The hostages emerged from their ordeal unscathed.
He spoke frankly about the ordeal.
The ordeal left her looking pale and drawn.
Polio died in December, 2010, weakened, Ginny believes, by the ordeal of defending himself.
News of the baby's ordeal was met with horror and pity by bloggers on Chinese sites.
